plan_adherence_notes: All 7 plan steps implemented exactly as specified: (1) factor operations (_factor_multiply, _factor_marginalize, _factor_normalize) at lines 404-453, (2) _initialize_potentials at lines 461-527, (3) wired into compile_network with CPT-conditional initialization, (4) Shafer-Shenoy belief propagation at lines 535-617 with BFS collect/distribute phases, (5) query_marginals at lines 717-738, (6) query_atomic_event with negation at lines 740-765, (7) tests updated with CPT fixtures and correctness tests. Files modified match plan: junction_tree.py and test_junction_tree.py only.
missed_questions: No caching of belief propagation results — every query re-runs BP from scratch. Fine for MVP but worth flagging for follow-up.
No validation of CPT consistency with graph structure (e.g., CPT parent keys matching declared edges). Malformed CPTs would silently produce incorrect results.
reason: All 7 plan steps implemented correctly. 31/31 tests pass including hand-computed correctness verification for A->B->C chain (P(B=b0)=0.62, P(C=c0)=0.586) and A->{B,C}->D diamond graphs. Research decisions (Shafer-Shenoy over Hugin, explicit elimination ordering only, standalone module, unconditional marginals only) were all well-justified and correctly implemented. Factor operations, potential initialization, and belief propagation are algorithmically sound. Query backend validates artifact integrity before execution. Code is clean and concentrated in 2 files (~785 LOC impl + ~518 LOC tests). Minor gaps (no BP caching, no CPT validation) are reasonable MVP deferrals.
